title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Kerala poll turnout rises to 79.7% after inclusion of service voters’ postal ballots, says CEO
content: The Chief Electoral Officer said 53,984 postal ballots had been issued to service voters, of which 20,028 had been received as of May 1.

title: Telangana sees more road accidents, fewer deaths in first quadrimester of 2026
content: At 9,313, road accidents hit a six-year high in the first four months this year, but fatalities dropped sharply by 15.3%, signalling gains from enforcement, trauma care and safety interventions across the State

title: BWSSB nets ₹12.15 crore under OTS scheme
content: The board has collected ₹12.15 crore from the first batch of 56,765 consumers who received settlement notices

title: New short Haj package likely to be popular among working pilgrims
content: The new option reduces the duration of the pilgrimage to 20 days, compared to the usual 40-45 day stay in Saudi Arabia. The first batch of pilgrims from Kerala availing themselves of the package will depart from Cochin International Airport on May 17

title: Vijay’s Velankanni visit cancelled; visuals of supporters chanting ‘TVK’ inside Basilica go viral
content: Addressing reporters at the party office in Karattur near Gobichettipalayam, Sengottaiyan said TVK party president Vijay is to emerge as a decisive leader and that 2026 verdict will be historic.

title: Ramagundam integrated coal mine project with 315 MT reserves gets green nod
content: The innovative mining project with two open cast and three underground mines has annual coal production target of 21 MT for 25 years

title: 284 companies to showcase defence tech at North Tech Symposium 2026
content: The symposium will serve as a key platform to showcase cutting-edge indigenous technologies designed to to address operational challenges faced by the armed forces

title: Change in plans: Bengaluru’s IT Corridor to be white-topped
content: Initially, the plan was to reconstruct a section of the road adjoining the metro pillars, while the other half was to undergo patchwork

title: MLA’s gunman withdrawn as he enters TTD-run temple with weapon
content: The gunman initially stays outside the Sri Vedanarayana Swamy temple at Nagalapuram, but rushes in along with the weapon on being called by the MLA to accept prasadam

title: Kerala Assembly Polls: LDF will return to power for third term, says M.V. Govindan
content: Taking a swipe at the Congress over the debate on the UDF’s Chief Ministerial face, Govindan says the Opposition camp was witnessing internal strife rather than discussion

title: State govt. initiates disciplinary action against suspended IAS officer N.Prasanth
content: Mr. Prasanth has in recent days appeared in chat shows in television and Youtube channels and made comments against the State government

title: Discuss and debate the Great Nicobar Project in Parliament: Congress
content: airam Ramesh says the government, rattled by Rahul Gandhi’s visit, had been forced to issue a clarification; it has failed to address serious ecological, tribal, financial and transparency concerns about the initiative, he adds

title: TDP activates its social media wing to publicise Mahanadu
content: Nellore district is hosting the annual conclave from May 27 to 29 for the first time since inception of the party

title: Kerala unlikely to enforce NMC directive on MBBS fees for four-and-a-half-years now
content: In the order issued on April 7, 2026, the NMC had said that collecting fee from students for the entire duration of the course for five years or five-and-a-half years would not be permitted

title: Parents call for summer holidays for Anganwadi Schools amid severe heatwave
content: Parents say it has become difficult to drop their children at Anganwadi schools and pick them up after half-day classes due to severe heatwaves

title: Urban Local Bodies collect 35% of annual property tax demand in April alone
content: Improved assessment coverage and expansion of municipal tax base have helped in mobilising ₹948.15 crore of the total demand of ₹2,683.50 crore at the start of the financial year itself, says MA&UD Principal Secretary Suresh Kumar

title: Mass marriages protect our culture, says Shivakumar
content: He was addressing newly wedded couples and others at a mass marriage programme at the Taluk Stadium in Sindhanur of Raichur district

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Tight security, traffic curbs in place as Thiruvananthapuram gears up for counting of votes
content: City police have operationalised cyber surveillance to track and act against provocative or misleading content on social media platforms

title: Integrated logistics planning done to promote  six horticulture crops in Andhra Pradesh
content: An assessment of the value chain, market linkages and infrastructure requirements of crops such as mango, banana, tomato, pomegranate, sweet lime and papaya is aimed at maximising returns for farmers and giving a boost to the economy

title: Only those who have the trust of Nehru-Gandhi family can become CM, says Bommai
content: There is no internal democracy in the Congress. It is a family party dominated by the Nehru-Gandhis, according to the BJP MP

title: SRM and The Hindu to host Future Career Conversations in Chennai on May 5
content: The programme will feature two sessions — ‘The Rejigged Employee’ and ‘The Learning Entrepreneur’ — focusing on adaptability, upskilling and innovation

title: Meeting reviews monsoon preparedness in Kozhikode
content: Measures will be taken to ensure water sanitation, the safety of school buildings and the cleanliness of wells and drinking water tanks in schools

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: ECI makes final preparations for counting votes at 62 centres across State
content: Chief Electoral Officer Archana Patnaik holds review meeting with with Election Commission officials ahead of counting day on May 4

title: Kerala Assembly polls: UDF allies have left CM choice to Congress, says AICC general secretary
content: Deepa Dasmunshi says discussions had been held with alliance partners on the issue and they had conveyed their willingness to abide by the Congress party’s decision

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: LDF, UDF keeping their fingers crossed in Kozhikode
content: Once a stronghold, the LDF is not predicting a similar performance, while the UDF is confident of a good show and the NDA expects to increase its vote share

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: 83.96% men, 86.2% women voted across State, says ECI
content: A total of 4,87,98,833 voters out of 5,73,43,291 turned up to exercise their franchise on April 23 in T.N. Assembly polls; total votes polled may go up as the current figures do not include postal ballots or senior citizens who voted through Form 12D

title: Heatwave grips Andhra Pradesh as Gudur records 46.2°C; rains in coastal pockets
content: 286 mandals report temperatures above 41°C; thunderstorms likely in parts of coastal Andhra as heatwave persists on May 4, prompting officials to urge precautions against extreme heat and lightning

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Vellappally Natesan hopes for LDF win, says BJP will open account in State
content: SNDP general secretary says LDF government’s welfare work among the poor will reflect in the results. The BJP is likely to win seats in Thiruvananthapuram and emerge as a corrective force in the State’s politics, he adds

title: Government to take up construction of 5,000 temples, says Minister
content: Mandipalli Ramprasad Reddy says that ₹13 crore has been mobilised through the Chief Minister’s relief fund and over ₹30 crore sanctioned, with 30 temples grounded and 70 more targeted by July

title: PM Modi, External Affairs Minister on major diplomatic outreach in May
content: The travels follow the tough message to Indian envoys on building the country’s image at the Heads of Mission conference

title: Two security staff members arrested after video shows them assaulting tribal man at Madhya Pradesh coal plant
content: A video shows one of the accused brutally beating up Annelal Baiga with a hockey stick as he pleads for him to stop; one of the bystanders is heard saying, “Leave the head and hit on the legs. Break his leg.”

title: Staying away from coal mines’ auction costs Singareni heavily
content: Delay in getting new mines affects coal quality with some coal linkage customers rejecting low-grade supplies, hits production

title: High security in place to avert post-poll clashes in Kozhikode
content: The counting process is scheduled to begin at 8 a.m. at three centres— JDT Group of Institutions, Vellimadukunnu; Government College, Madappally; and St. Mary’s Higher Secondary School, Koodathayi— under tight police protection

title: Ahead Of Counting, Assam BJP Office Gears Up For 'Victory' Celebration
content: While preparations are in full swing, the official results are yet to be announced, and counting is scheduled for tomorrow.

title: Cheap, Invisible, Accurate: Hezbollah's Deadly New Weapon Against Israel
content: Israel believes Hezbollah sources the base drones from China or Iran before attaching a grenade or similar explosive device to each one.

title: US-Returned Man Jumps From Gurugram High-Rise After Marital Dispute, Dies: Cops
content: A 45-year-old man who had recently returned from the US died allegedly by suicide after jumping from the eighth floor of a high-rise building in Sector 65 here, police said on Sunday.

title: Beating The Blackout: The Clandestine Network Smuggling Starlink Into Iran
content: Last month, Iranian media reported that four people including two foreign nationals had been arrested for "importing satellite internet equipment"

title: AAP vs Rebel MPs: Both Camps To Meet President Murmu On Tuesday
content: The rebel MPs are likely to argue that administrative actions against them are politically motivated following their exit from AAP and subsequent alignment with the BJP.

title: Stripped, Chased, Assaulted: Nigerian 'Rape Festival" Causes Global Outrage
content: The incident reportedly took place during the Alue-Do festival, a traditional event associated with prayers and blessings, especially for fertility.

title: Counting Of Votes For Nagaland's Koridang Assembly Bypoll Tomorrow
content: All arrangements have been made for the counting of votes for the Koridang Assembly Constituency byelection in Nagaland's Mokokchung district, scheduled to be held on May 4, an official said on Sunday.

title: Vote Counting For Karnataka's Bagalkot, Davanagere South Bypolls Tomorrow
content: Counting of votes for bypolls to two assembly constituencies in Karnataka -- Bagalkot and Davanagere South -- will be held on May 4, amid tight security, election officials said Sunday.

title: 2 Dead After Explosion At House In Southern England
content: Police, who have declared it a major incident, said they were not treating it as a suspected terrorist incident.

title: 'Impossible Operation Or Bad Deal': Iran Guards' 2 Options For Trump
content: The Guards' intelligence organisation said that US President Donald "Trump must choose between 'an impossible operation or a bad deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran'", in an online post carried by state television.

title: "Death Was In Front Of Us": Jabalpur Boat Tragedy Survivors Recount Horror
content: A boat cruise accident at the Bargi Dam turned moments of joy into tragedy, leaving several families devastated. What began as a leisure outing quickly became a life-and-death situation as passengers struggled to survive.

title: Joram Aniya Becomes First Full-Time NITI Aayog Member From Arunachal
content: Dr Joram Anya is the first woman from the Nyishi community to earn a PhD and the first one in Arunachal Pradesh to complete the degree in Hindi.

title: 'Self-Funded Visit': NDTV Tracks Man In Mamata Banerjee's 'Outsider' Video
content: Deep Prajapati said his visit to West Bengal was self-funded and aimed at documenting what he described as the "ground reality" of the state.

title: Flier Vomits Twice, Jumps Off Moving Plane's Emergency Exit At Chennai Airport
content: An official said the passenger on the Sharjah-Chennai Air Arabia flight complained of a vomiting sensation and is believed to have vomited twice on board.

title: On Camera, 17-Year-Old Dies After Electrocution During Rain In Bengaluru
content: There was panic at the scene as bystanders rushed to help. However, they struggled to intervene due to the live current.

title: Video: Diljit Dosanjh Asks Pro-Khalistanis Waving Flag At Canada Concert To Leave
content: Singer Diljit Dosanjh's concerts have in the past seen pro-Khalistani flag-waving people try to disrupt the events

title: Deadly rally, deleted video, 90L 'ghosts' & fish: Inside the high-octane chaos of 2026 poll campaign
content: The 2026 Indian Assembly Elections showcased a nation in transition, marked by regional pride, welfare economics, and celebrity influence. Tragedies, controversial rhetoric, and mass voter roll deletions defined campaigns across states, leading to high voter turnout and intense political theatre.

title: Israel doubles air force fleet with mega US jet deal: What is 'Shield of Israel' plan?
content: Israel's defense ministry has approved a multi-billion dollar deal to purchase two new combat squadrons of advanced F-35 and F-15IA fighter jets from Lockheed Martin and Boeing. This significant expansion of air power aims to bolster the country's long-term military capability and air superiority, with a focus on domestic production and technological advancement.

title: Seats low, stakes high: Is BJP expanding quietly in Kerala?
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party is making inroads in Kerala, challenging the state's traditional two-front political system. Recent elections show the BJP gaining ground in specific constituencies, turning contests into triangular battles. This shift is evident in Lok Sabha and local body polls, indicating a gradual disruption of the established LDF-UDF dominance. The party focuses on select seats for strategic growth.

title: As Accenture rolls out Microsoft Copilot to staff, CEO says teams doing higher-value work
content: Microsoft's Copilot 365 AI assistant is now powering Accenture's workforce of 743,000 employees, marking the largest enterprise deal for the chatbot. This significant adoption by Accenture, a leading AI adopter, highlights Microsoft's strategy to boost paid Copilot subscriptions. Early results show employees completing routine tasks up to 15 times faster, with major productivity gains reported.

title: WFI refuses to shift venue linked to Brij Bhushan despite Vinesh Phogat's concerns
content: "If Vinesh is worried about her safety, let me assure her that I am taking personal guarantee of that. Also, we have UWW-approved referees who officiate and all trial bouts are recorded, so there is no chance of any bias," Sanjay Singh was quoted as saying by news agency PTI.

title: ‘I'm vegetarian, need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: How a weaker dollar is quietly making life more expensive for Americans
content: A declining U.S. dollar, down approximately 10% since Donald Trump's return to the White House, is quietly increasing the cost of everyday life for Americans. This weakening currency shrinks purchasing power, making imported goods and foreign travel more expensive, while benefiting large multinational exporters.

title: Watch: Diljit confronts fans waving pro-Khalistan flags, halts Canada concert
content: Diljit Dosanjh paused his Calgary concert amid pro-Khalistan flags, addressing criticism. He defended his efforts to highlight Punjab's issues on platforms like Kaun Banega Crorepati and The Tonight Show, stating his focus remains on his homeland. This follows past controversy with Sikhs For Justice over his interactions with Amitabh Bachchan.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 45: Ranveer Singh’s film eyes Rs 1790 cr globally
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1,138.54 crore in India net collections after 45 days. The film's 7th Saturday saw a significant surge, earning Rs 2.40 crore, pushing its gross worldwide total to Rs 1,788.32 crore. This success comes despite new releases, proving its enduring appeal.

title: After over 20K layoffs, how Oracle's 'promise' to Sam Altman is hurting banks across US
content: Oracle faces a significant bank-debt challenge due to its massive data centre expansion for OpenAI, reportedly requiring $300 billion. Lenders struggle with exposure limits, impacting their ability to finance these projects.  This comes as Oracle also plans substantial job cuts, highlighting financial pressures despite efforts to secure funding.

title: Chakravarthy, Narine script SRH collapse as KKR end Hyderabad's winning run
content: Varun Chakravarthy took 3 for 36 and Sunil Narine claimed 2 for 31 as SRH were bowled out for 165 in 19 overs. KKR chased the target with 10 balls remaining to register their third straight win.

title: 'Impossible military op or ...': Iran mocks US, says Trump has only 2 options left
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps stated the US faces a choice between an "impossible" military operation or a "bad deal," as diplomatic efforts have stalled. Tehran reportedly submitted a 14-point proposal to Pakistan, outlining a 30-day framework to resolve issues, including US troop withdrawal and sanctions relief.

title: Manish Pandey scripts history, becomes only third player after Kohli & Rohit to ...
content: The 36-year-old replaced Ramandeep Singh in the KKR XI for their ninth match of the season. He had been bought for Rs 75 lakh at the auction and had not played earlier in the season.

title: What will TVK's Vijay be: Kejriwal of Delhi or Kishor of Bihar?
content: Actor-turned-politician Vijay Thalapathy's new party, TVK, has entered Tamil Nadu's political arena, aiming to disrupt the long-standing duopoly of DMK and AIADMK. While exit polls suggest a notable debut, projections vary, with some indicating TVK could act as a spoiler by splitting anti-incumbency votes.

title: Laura Loomer warns Candace Owens of lawsuit in Erika Kirk row
content: Candace Owens is facing a potential lawsuit threat from Laura Loomer after refusing to retract claims involving Erika Kirk and the 2025 killing of Charlie Kirk. Owens insists she stands by her statements, while Loomer warned legal action is imminent. The dispute has intensified with a separate lawsuit filed by Brian Harpole, escalating tensions.

title: 'Govt will be responsible': Phogat wary of competing at venue under Brij Bhushan influence
content: In a video message ahead of her return after nearly 18 months, Vinesh said the competition, to be held at a venue linked to Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh, could see results influenced by people close to the former Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) chief.

title: Kerala's power 'cycle' put to test: Will Left keep rolling or UDF take the seat?
content: Estimates vary, but the range broadly suggests the UDF could cross the halfway mark in the 140-member House, with projections spanning from around 70 seats at the lower end to close to 90 at the higher end. In contrast, the LDF is largely seen falling short, with projections indicating a ceiling below a simple majority. If these projections hold, the outcome would carry significance beyond a routine change of government.

title: Dianna Russini pressured The Athletic in Vrabel scandal: Insider claims
content: Dianna Russini faces fresh scrutiny after insider Mike Florio suggested she may have pushed The Athletic to publicly support her during the Mike Vrabel controversy. Early statements defending her are now being questioned, especially as investigations continue. With Russini stepping away and uncertainty growing, the situation raises concerns about media influence, internal pressure, and credibility within NFL reporting circles.

title: IPL: Sunil Narine scripts history, becomes the first overseas player to...
content: Sunil Narine achieved a historic milestone, becoming the third bowler and first overseas player to claim 200 IPL wickets. His crucial spell, alongside Varun Chakravarthy's, spearheaded Kolkata Knight Riders' remarkable comeback against Sunrisers Hyderabad. After a blazing start by SRH, KKR's spinners triggered a dramatic collapse, restricting the opposition to 165.

title: FPIs continue to exit Indian markets, sold Rs 60,847 cr worth equities in April
content: Foreign investors are exiting Indian stocks, with outflows reaching Rs 60,847 crore in April, following a massive Rs 1,17,775 crore sell-off in March. This trend signals sustained weakness in overseas investor sentiment, as global capital shifts towards Asian economies benefiting from the AI investment boom, particularly South Korea and Taiwan.

title: 'I'm one of six victims harassed by Brij Bhushan': Vinesh Phogat drops bombshell
content: "Due to certain circumstances, I want to tell you all something. I did not want to speak while the case is still pending... But I want to say that I myself am one of those six victims who filed a complaint, and our testimonies are still ongoing."

title: Why Sooryavanshi was called 'unprofessional'? Jitesh Sharma reveals truth
content: Wicketkeeper Jitesh Sharma clarified his "unprofessional" comment about young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, explaining it stemmed from their close friendship. Sharma stressed that the 15-year-old is a typical teenager who enjoys simple pleasures. He urged against placing undue pressure on the youngster, advocating for him to enjoy his childhood while focusing on cricket.

title: Puducherry assembly polls: From Thattanchavady to Raj Bhavan - key seats to watch
content: Puducherry's vote counting begins Monday for its 30 constituencies, a high-stakes election with record 89.83% turnout. The ruling NDA, led by AINRC, faces a strong challenge from the Congress-led SPA and emerging player TVK. Key constituencies like Thattanchavady and Mannadipet are closely watched.

title: Laugh in the face of darkness: How humor became humanity's oldest coping mechanism
content: Amidst hardship, dark humor emerges as a vital survival tool, offering a defiant spark against despair. This article explores how gallows humor, distinct from denial, helps individuals and communities cope with overwhelming realities. It highlights Gen Z's use of memes as a modern form of crisis comedy, underscoring laughter's profound psychological and biological benefits.

title: Crude beyond $120: Oil prices touch four-year peak, here's what analysts are saying
content: Global oil prices surged to a four-year high amid escalating US-Iran tensions and fears of a Strait of Hormuz closure, impacting gasoline prices and broader economies. Analysts warn of a potential global recession due to supply disruptions, with shortages already affecting various goods. Experts predict further price hikes until the strait reopens.

title: 'Not surprising': BJP MP Sandeep Pathak on 2 FIRs filed against him
content: Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak stated he has not received information about FIRs filed against him, suspecting political misuse. Punjab Police registered two non-bailable FIRs against Pathak after he switched from AAP to BJP. BJP and SAD leaders condemned the actions as political vendetta.

title: Candace Owens edits Erika Kirk AI gun thumbnail after backlash
content: Candace Owens is facing renewed backlash after quietly editing a controversial thumbnail linked to her latest episode. Critics claim the changes were subtle and failed to address the core issue, while supporters continue to defend her intent. The episode was also renamed and added to a series, raising further questions. The controversy has grown alongside reports of legal action against conspiracy-driven content tied to Erika Kirk and Charlie Kirk.

title: Watch: How Scientology churches across US became the latest target for Gen Z viral stunts
content: Around 50 teenagers forced their way into a Church of Scientology building in New York City, damaging property and injuring a security guard. The incident, linked to a social media trend called 'Scientology speed running,' involved coordinated trespass and aggression. Police are investigating similar disruptions reported in Los Angeles.

title: Minecraft Bedrock 26.30.25/26 beta: How to download latest version
content: Minecraft Bedrock 26.30.25/26 beta and preview is now available, featuring content from the upcoming Chaos Cubed update, including Sulfur Cube Archetypes and Geysers. This beta allows players to test new features before their official release. The article details how to download and install the beta on PC, Xbox, PlayStation, Android, and iOS devices.

title: Kerala verdict: Pinarayi Vijayan's welfare rule - will it fare well or get a farewell?
content: Kerala's upcoming election sees the LDF government banking on its development model and welfare gains for a third consecutive term, a feat rarely achieved due to anti-incumbency. Despite high SDG rankings, the state faces challenges in gender equality and economic growth, while migration and wage gaps emerge as key voter concerns.

title: Leather, footwear sector seeks duty relief as Middle East crisis drives up input costs
content: India's leather and footwear industry faces a 40-60% surge in input costs due to the Middle East crisis, impacting petroleum-based materials and imports. Industry representatives are urging the government for import duty exemptions on raw materials and machinery to mitigate these rising expenses and strengthen domestic manufacturing.

title: 'MPs who switched to BJP targeted': Chadha claims political vendetta, to meet Prez on May 5
content: Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha is set to meet President Droupadi Murmu on May 5 to voice concerns regarding the alleged misuse of state machinery in Punjab. He plans to highlight political vendetta against former AAP MPs who recently joined the BJP. Chadha, along with three other MPs, will present these issues to the President.

title: India gets 'Drishti': An unblinking eye in the sky on Pak, China borders; PM Modi hails milestone
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More
